title: For the price, this is a terrific smart watch!
text:
The first thing to wrap around your head is that this watch doesn't sync up with a lot of Western apps, such as MyFitnessPal. I hope that changes in the future, because this is a terrific piece of gear for a very competitive price.

The other thing is that your customer support (and much of the manual/app data) is translated from Chinese, so it comes across as odd. (For instance, after stopping the exercise timer, you'll get a message stating "Does it complete the exercise?" It just sounds weird out loud.) However, when I contacted them with a question, I received a very detailed answer within 24 hours.

There are the standard problems with the pedometer, just as with any smart watch, in that it tracks any arm movement as a step. The sleep tracker is pretty accurate and monitors your heart rate to track normal sleep and deep sleep patterns. The battery life is also terrific, although I'll admit that I don't have a constant heart rate monitor going, which I assume wo

title: Needs the App more than I thought it would.
text:
It's a big display. On the app you choose different layouts or designs.
It's a good watch.
I am not a big fitness person, but it tracks my steps, heart rate.
Easy to charge-magnetic connection, easy break away without damaging watch or charger.
I turned off getting texts to the watch. Aside from the print was too small to read, it was annoying and a battery drain. I am connected enough.
I was disappointed that the only way to start/stop BP is with the phone app. I hoped it could be accomplished on the watch itself.
I don't wear it to bed, so I don't track sleep. I find it comfortable during the day, but to bulky for sleep. The app is OK, not complicated, but I am not using it to its full advantage.
I wanted a reliable watch, large display, that gave me some fitness tracking and was waterproof. I got that. If you are a very serious and active fitness person, you may want to invest in something higher end.

title: OK as a basic activity tracker/watch.
text:
Update - 12/8/22

I do have a couple of updates for my review. I went from 4 to 3 stars, mostly due to durability issues. Over the 5 months I have had this watch, it has gotten scratched very easily. Granted, this was typically on the type of thing that would also scratch anything else made of glass, such as taping a painted brick wall, but the scratches also lead to a crack in the watch face. The crack is currently about 2/3rds of the way down the screen diagonally. The band also tore off. Replacement bands are easy to find and cheap, and I do wear the watch in the gym and other more rigorous activities, but still, I'm not running triathlons or anything like that. Also, and I'm not sure if this is related, but the sleep tracking doesn't always work. I can't really say why. For sure, it doesn't record naps, but also, it will decide I'm awake and then stop tracking sleep. It will say that I slept for 2 hours when I really was asleep for 7-8 hours. I would say it's good for abo

title: Great Watch for Someone on a Tight Budget
text: This is a great starter watch and I love the different face options. I have found the heart rate, blood pressure and oxygen levels to be pretty accurate. I visit the doctor's office often and I always compare my results with theirs and the the results are pretty much on point. I feel that there needs to be more options for exercise because some of us can't do traditional exercise for various reasons some some additional options such as chores around the house or a general exercise category would be great. I am a teacher and I am still moving boxes from my storage unit to my classroom and from my home to the classroom but there is not a category for that type of work. I am also doing work in my house which would qualify as exercise but there is not a category for that either. One of my main issues with the watch is that it does it consistently keep track of my sleep which is something that is very important to me. It was great in te beginning but now it does not track my sleep every nig

title: Inaccurate Junk
text: Every little arm movement triggers multiple steps. I apparently walked nearly a whole lap just drinking coffee while working. Also, the heart rate monitor is wildly off. I calibrated it with a stopwatch, and instead of the 65 bpm I was counting, it regularly shows my around 100 bpm. Don't make my same mistake. Save your money for a good watch. Expect to pay over 100 USD for a usable watch.

title: Very nice smart watch
text: I really wanted a smart watch and this one was recommended by a colleague. I decided this try it. It was the perfect size for me. Fairly easy to set up and pair with my smart phone using the app. It has a BP function but not sure how accurate it is but the other functions are acceptable. I have not had It long or wore it as yet so not sure how long the battery lasts.

title: Best watch ever
text: I was in the market for a watch that tracked my steps. This watch was way more than that, I am inpressed. I can't believe all the features you get for a watch that cost 40bucks. Why do people buy all those expensive ones from Samsung and Apple. This watch does it all. Super comfy with the silicone band. Battery life is awesome. Super excited about this watch.

title: Not that accurate in measuring BP and your steps
text:
It is ok for basic functions. But is is wildly inaccurate with measuring blood pressure. I tested it in the doctors office and it was about 15 or 20 points different from I the doctors device. Even when I test BP in the watch within a couple of minutes, the readings are off by five to ten points. My blood pressure certainly doesn’t change that much within a two minute span.

Also, measuring the steps is inaccurate. It tells me that I am walking steps even while sitting at my desk for hours. For example, it sent me an alert telling me “You reached your goal “. This being despite sitting at my desk most of the day.

So, it tells time fine and heart rate seems accurate but I would not purchase this watch if I were you. I got it because I did not want to pay $200 to $300 for an Apple watch. Don’t get this if you want accurate monitoring of your steps and blood pressure.

title: It's 99% accurate if worn on right hand
text:
I have had this product for a few months now,
Pros- tracks steps, bp, step counter, heart rate, workout sessions, and app is easy to download and easy to set up and use. Also, battery life lasts 4 days
Cons- heart rate accurate, BP is not even close, workout tracker could have a few extra options.

Every fit bit I have bought the step tracker works and if you move your arms alot it tracks those as steps too.
Left wrist- well it don't track all the steps because my left wrist doesn't swing as much as my right
Right wrist- it is my dominant hand but I wear the watch on this wrist because the left wrist doesn't seem as accurate. All fit bits are this way. But you only need to worry about the fact that the watch has great qualities to it needed I do recommend this watch for all features!
Blood pressure (bp) tracker is the only thing not as accurate the rest is as accurate as a watch can be. Also you can try using Google fit it's free don't require a watch

title: Super short battery life just one to two days
text: Battrey only last one to two day even if you turn all the smart features off.  Get a different on!!

title: Nice purchase for the price!
text:
I received my watch the same day I ordered it. So great. And the watch is very nice for the price. I gave 4 stars because the watch does jump around too quickly at times from screen to screen.
The feature I like is text messages have alot of space. Also many cool clock faces to choose from. Donerton nailed it!
Buy it you won't be disappointed

title: Honest review as of 9/28/2022... You get what you pay for
text:
Had the watch since November of 2021.
Pros:
- Accurate time
- the ability to Customize watch faces... even with a picture!
- Could be mistaken for an apple watch at quick glance
- Long battery life. I charge this watch like once every 4-5 days. And wear it everyday

Cons:
-ALL of the SENSORS are INACCURATE. HEART RATE, OXYGEN LEVEL,BLOOD PRESSURE, SLEEP and STEPS are all INACCURATE. If you're expecting the accuracy of a name brand smart watch, this $30 watch doesn't do it.
- Weather doesn't update
- Only 3 Notifications are allowed. If the text is too long, you can't read it all.
- basic interface
- the app [heroband iii] sucks
- Camera function is stupid. There's no camera on the watch itself. So all the camera function does is act as a shutter button for your phone. Kind of a pointless feature

It's okay if you're getting this watch as a placeholder or starter watch before getting something better

title: Nice watch, but
text: Nice watch. But, hard to I stall.

title: Set up
text: Havent had it long enough to test everything out but the set up is super easy. Go to google play store get heroband111 app and your on your way. If this old lady can do it anyone can.

title: Selling Customer Info?
text: The app required to link the watch to the phone required my credit card information, although the watch was paid  for and the app was "free".

title: Don’t too small
text: We purchased this based on the reviews. I have an inexpensive semi-smart watch/fitness tracker, my husband wanted one that was Rectangular. Based on the stellar reviews we ordered this one. It has two flaws; one when you turn it up so you can see the watch face it lights up and then within two seconds the screen shrinks down to a smaller watch face that’s hard to read the other problem is that the text messages do come through on it but the font is so tiny it’s unreadable. Maybe if you’re 20 years old this will work fine. We are 50 & 60 and the font was just not large enough for us.

title: Make Sure it is Compatible with your Phone!
text: Unfortunately, this was not compatible with my sons phone so we had to return it. He has an AT&T Calypso.

title: Easy to set up and use.  Like knowing my steps count to better health.
text: It was easy to download the program to my Android  phone ti get started.  Love knowing my everyday steps count towards my progress to my overall better heakth.

title: Fitness tracker
text: Not a bad smart watch per say its good for the price u can get a host of notifications and track lots of health features/ stats.

title: I returned it!
text: It would not even let me set the time without purchasing the app.  I just needed a quick watch.

title: Quality is p[oor ,but i need it replaced it gets caught on clothing ,can you replace it,
text: Price good but now gets caught on material i wear its defective, caught on clothing

title: Great price love the design
text: It's got my watch in the mail today and I really like it. It was easy to set up and I love the design of the watch. For the steps to work you have to take the plastic off the watch.

title: Doesn't work
text: Connected to Bluetooth but nothing on the phone worked except the time- which was a waste of mine and my money 🙄 will pay extra for an apple

title: For the money not baf
text: Can’t get it to track my sleep at all. Steps are not exactly correct but close. Mainly use it for phone and text info. Heart rate and blood pressure are fairly correct. Still love the itouch watch better but foe the money not bad
